methods.description: Collection of fecal and museum samples from various sites across Africa to study the effect of Pleistocene refugia and environmental heterogeneity on population structure.

steps.description: A total of 2074 samples (2040 fecal samples and 34 museum and zoo tissues) were collected from 46 sites across nine countries in Africa.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fecal and museum samples from various sites in Africa

steps.description: At each site, fresh fecal pellets (< 24 h) were sampled opportunistically along a transect line that followed a different compass bearing each day.
steps.searchable_summary: sample fresh fecal pellets along transect lines

steps.description: Replicate samples from the same individual were eliminated on the basis of their multi-locus genotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: eliminate replicate samples based on genotypes

steps.description: One to four pellets were placed into a vial containing either 5 g of silica gel beads or a vial containing 1.2 ml of RNAlater.
steps.searchable_summary: store fecal pellets in silica gel or RNAlater

steps.description: For each fecal sample the following information was collected: site, GPS coordinates and major habitat type in which it was collected.
steps.searchable_summary: record site, GPS, and habitat type for each sample

methods.description: Extraction of genomic DNA from fecal and museum samples using specific kits and protocols to ensure high yield and prevent contamination.

steps.description: Genomic DNA extraction was carried out using the DNA stool Minikit (Qiagen, CA).
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA using DNA stool Minikit

steps.description: DNA was either extracted from teeth or from muscle preserved in ethanol as previously described.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA from teeth or muscle preserved in ethanol

steps.description: Several precautions were taken to minimize the risk of DNA contamination. Extraction blanks and a negative (no DNA) control were included in PCR reactions.
steps.searchable_summary: include controls to prevent DNA contamination

steps.description: Fecal DNA extractions and preparation of PCR reactions was carried out in a designated room where only samples from degraded DNA were allowed.
steps.searchable_summary: perform DNA extractions in designated room

steps.description: Only filter tips were used in the preparation of samples for extraction and all work spaces were cleaned with UV light, ethanol and/or bleach prior to PCR amplification.
steps.searchable_summary: use filter tips and clean workspaces before PCR
